# Claude Code prompt — [Fix] Merge duplicate Quote edit tabs (PR-9 UI follow-up)

*Paste below the line. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-29.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## Background — what went wrong

PR #253 (the PR-9 UI scope-items redesign) was supposed to redesign the EXISTING Quote edit sub-tabs. Instead, it created a SECOND set of tabs alongside the existing ones.

Current state of the Quote → Edit view shows **duplicated tabs**:

- **Existing edit tabs** (the ones we want to keep):
  `Cost Summary` · `Scope items` · `Provisional Sums` · `Cost Options` · `Assumptions` · `Exclusions` · `Terms & Conditions` · `Preview`
- **Duplicate tab strip added by PR #253** (the one we want to remove, keeping its content):
  `Cost Summary` · `Assumptions` · `Exclusions` · `Terms & Conditions` · `Generate Quote`

Owner's words: *"there is a double up on the tabs: Cost Summary, Assumptions, Exclusions. The system misunderstood where I wanted the changes to occur. it was supposed to be on the existing tabs. can you merge them?"*

The fix is to **merge** the duplicate tabs into the existing tabs — preserve all PR #253 functionality (discipline grouping, +Add row, +Add discipline modal, DnD, Simple-mode editable descriptions), but rendered inside the EXISTING tab structure rather than a parallel one.

## Operating rules

1. **Single branch:** `fix/merge-duplicate-quote-tabs`. Stay on it.
2. **Work in phases.** Each phase ends in a commit and a push, prefixed `[pr13-phase-N]`.
3. **After each phase, run `pnpm build` and `pnpm lint`.** Both must pass.
4. **Hard ABORT:** if you read/re-read the same file 3 times in a row without a successful Edit, STOP and report. Don't loop. The file you're editing is `ClientQuotesPanel.tsx` — it's >80KB and Edit-call whitespace mismatches are common. Copy the surrounding context fresh from a new Read each time.
5. **Do NOT reason about file watchers or external processes.** If Edit calls aren't sticking, it's a whitespace mismatch in your `old_string`, every time.
6. **Do NOT batch all phases into one commit.**

## Phase 0 — Resume detection + branch setup

```
git fetch origin
git log --all --oneline | grep -E "\[pr13-phase-" | head -10
```

- No matches → start from Phase 1 on a fresh branch off `main`:

  git checkout -b fix/merge-duplicate-quote-tabs
  ```

  git pull
  ```

## Phase 1 — Inventory the duplicate tab structure

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr13-phase-1\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** Read-only investigation phase. No code changes. The output is a written summary committed as a doc note, so the next phase has a clear map.

1. In `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/ClientQuotesPanel.tsx`, find the **existing** edit-mode tab list. Search for the string `"Scope items"` (or `'Scope items'`). The array around it is the canonical tab list. Note its variable name, its render location, and the active-tab state hook name.
2. Find the **duplicate** tab list PR #253 introduced. Search for the string `"Generate Quote"` (or `'Generate Quote'`) — that string is unique to the duplicate strip. Note its variable name, render location, and active-tab state hook.
3. For each duplicate tab (`Cost Summary`, `Assumptions`, `Exclusions`, `Terms & Conditions`, `Generate Quote`), identify the JSX block that renders when that duplicate tab is active. Note line ranges.
4. For each EXISTING tab with the same name (`Cost Summary`, `Assumptions`, `Exclusions`, `Terms & Conditions`), identify the JSX block that renders when it's active. Note line ranges.
5. Write a short note `docs/pr-prompts/pr-13-tab-merge-inventory.md` with:
   - Existing tab list variable + array + render block line ranges per tab.
   - Duplicate tab list variable + array + render block line ranges per tab.
   - A merge plan: for each duplicate, which existing tab it should merge into, and whether the existing tab's current content should be REPLACED (if the duplicate's content is the new/correct version) or COMBINED with the duplicate's content.
6. Commit:
   ```
   git add docs/pr-prompts/pr-13-tab-merge-inventory.md
   git commit -m "[pr13-phase-1] docs: inventory duplicate Quote edit tabs + merge plan"
   git push -u origin fix/merge-duplicate-quote-tabs
   ```

**Stop. Verify the inventory note is on origin before moving on.**

---

## Phase 2 — Merge `Cost Summary` content (PR-9 simple-mode editable descriptions into existing tab)

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr13-phase-2\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** Move the simple-mode editable-description logic from the duplicate `Cost Summary` tab into the existing `Cost Summary` tab. Then delete the duplicate `Cost Summary` from the duplicate tab array (do NOT delete the whole duplicate strip yet — that's the last phase).

1. Per the inventory note, copy the editable-description rendering (the `<input>` bound to `costLine.displayDescription` with blur PATCH) from the duplicate `Cost Summary` render block.
2. Paste it into the existing `Cost Summary` render block, replacing the current read-only description cell whenever `quote.detailLevel === "Simple"` AND `isEditing` is true.
3. The SCOPE column stays read-only. The AMOUNT column behaviour is unchanged.
4. Remove `"Cost Summary"` from the duplicate tab array.
5. Delete the now-orphaned JSX block that rendered the duplicate `Cost Summary` content.
6. Build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr13-phase-2] fix: merge Cost Summary tab content into existing tab"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 3 — Merge `Assumptions` content

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr13-phase-3\]"` returns true.

1. Identify what content the duplicate `Assumptions` tab renders. If it's the same list editor as the existing tab, just remove the duplicate (the existing tab is fine). If the duplicate has new functionality (e.g. add/remove buttons, override highlighting), port that into the existing tab.
2. Remove `"Assumptions"` from the duplicate tab array.
3. Delete the orphaned JSX block.
4. Build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr13-phase-3] fix: merge Assumptions tab into existing tab"
   git push
   ```

## Phase 5 — Reconcile `Terms & Conditions` (PR #252 already added the canonical one)

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr13-phase-5\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** PR #252 already added a `Terms & Conditions` tab to the EXISTING tab strip with the full clause editor, override-highlight, and revert. PR #253 also added a `Terms & Conditions` tab to the DUPLICATE strip. The existing one is canonical — remove the duplicate.

1. Confirm the existing `Terms & Conditions` tab renders the PR #252 clause editor (clause list, textarea per clause, OverrideField wrapper, ↺ revert button). If it does not — port whatever it's missing from the duplicate.
2. Remove `"Terms & Conditions"` from the duplicate tab array.
3. Delete the orphaned JSX block.
4. Build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr13-phase-5] fix: drop duplicate Terms & Conditions tab; keep PR-252 canonical"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 6 — Decide where `Generate Quote` lives

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr13-phase-6\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** `Generate Quote` is the only duplicate tab without a same-named counterpart in the existing strip. Decide whether it belongs:
- As a button in the quote header (next to Save/Cancel), OR
- As an action inside the existing `Preview` tab.

Owner preference: button in the quote header next to Save/Cancel. Use that unless the existing Preview tab already has the same functionality, in which case keep it there and drop the duplicate.

1. Read the duplicate `Generate Quote` render block. Note what it does (typically: triggers PDF generation via an existing endpoint, possibly downloads the file).
2. Move that handler + button into the quote header area (the same area where Save/Cancel/Edit render). Place the button to the right of Save, only visible when NOT editing.
3. Remove `"Generate Quote"` from the duplicate tab array.
4. Delete the orphaned JSX block.
5. Build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr13-phase-6] fix: move Generate Quote action into header; drop duplicate tab"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 7 — Delete the empty duplicate tab strip

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr13-phase-7\]"` returns true.

By this point the duplicate tab array is empty (or close to it). Remove:

1. The duplicate tab array variable and its `useState` for the active duplicate tab.
2. The JSX that rendered the duplicate tab strip (`<nav>` / `<div role="tablist">` / whatever wraps the duplicate buttons).
3. Any imports that are now unused (run `pnpm lint` and let it flag them).
4. Any helper functions that only the duplicate strip referenced.
5. Build + lint. **Pay close attention to lint warnings about unused vars — fix them inline.**
6. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr13-phase-7] chore: remove empty duplicate tab strip + dead code"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 8 — Verify the existing `Scope items` tab still has all PR #253 functionality

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr13-phase-8\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** Read-only verification phase. PR #253 was supposed to redesign the `Scope items` tab (discipline grouping, +Add row, +Add discipline modal, DnD, label read-only, Source column removed). Make sure all of that is present on the EXISTING `Scope items` tab — not on a duplicate.

1. Open the EXISTING `Scope items` tab render. Verify:
   - Label column is read-only (not an `<input>`).
   - Source column does NOT exist.
   - Items are grouped by discipline with `{disciplineName} ({count})` section headers.
   - Each section has a `+ Add row` button.
   - Below the table there's a top-level `+ Add discipline` button that opens a modal.
   - Rows have a drag handle and are sortable within a discipline.
   - Cross-discipline drop PATCHes `quoteDiscipline`.
2. If any of those are missing, port them from wherever they currently live (could be a leftover helper in the duplicate code path that didn't get moved). Commit the port:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr13-phase-8] fix: ensure Scope items tab has all PR-253 functionality"
   git push
   ```
3. If everything is already correct, commit a no-op verification note:
   ```
   git commit --allow-empty -m "[pr13-phase-8] verify: Scope items tab confirmed canonical"
   git push
   ```

## Final step — Open the PR

**Already done if:** a PR exists on GitHub for branch `fix/merge-duplicate-quote-tabs`.

Once all phase commits are on origin:

- Open PR via `gh pr create`.
- Title: `[Fix] Merge duplicate Quote edit tabs (PR-9 UI follow-up)`
- Body:
  - Reference PR #253 as the upstream that introduced the duplicate strip.
  - Reference PR #252 as the source of the canonical `Terms & Conditions` tab.
  - List the eight phases with one-line summaries.
  - Call out that **no functionality is removed** — only the duplicate tab containers are removed; their content is preserved in the existing tabs.
- Reviewer: `GH-Mantova`.
- **Do NOT auto-merge.** Owner verifies live on IS-T020-R1.

## Tests

Light coverage per phase is fine. At minimum:

- Edit mode renders exactly ONE tab strip (snapshot or DOM-count test).
- Existing `Cost Summary` tab in Simple mode renders an editable description input.
- `Scope items` tab renders discipline-grouped sections with `+ Add row` per section.
- `Terms & Conditions` tab renders the clause editor (textarea + OverrideField).
- `Generate Quote` button appears in the header (not as a tab) when NOT editing.

## Scope — do NOT do

- Do NOT change the backend (`scope-of-works.service.ts`, routes, DTOs, Prisma schema). Pure frontend merge.
- Do NOT touch the `Preview` tab unless it's where `Generate Quote` ends up (Phase 6 decision).
- Do NOT redesign anything — this is a structural merge, not a feature change.
- Do NOT make a single Edit call that rewrites >100 lines of `ClientQuotesPanel.tsx`. Split.

## If you get stuck

Stop and report:
- Which phase you're on.
- The file's current state (`git diff HEAD --stat`).
- The error or whitespace mismatch.
- What you've tried.

DO NOT loop on the same Edit call. DO NOT reason about "external processes reverting edits" — it's a whitespace mismatch in your `old_string`, every time. Re-Read fresh, copy the exact surrounding context, retry once, and if it still fails, report.
